Article.ArticleTitle: Synergistic enhancement of gelling properties of low-salt shrimp surimi via ultra-high pressure and egg white protein integration: A multiscale structural approach.

Abstract.AbstractText: This study evaluates the synergistic effects of ultra-high pressure (UHP, 290 MPa) and egg white protein (EWP, 37.5%) on Litopenaeus vannamei myofibrillar protein (MP) gelation under low-salt (1.5% NaCl) conditions. The combined treatment (U-E) achieved a gel strength of 278.03 g·mm, exhibiting whiteness, texture, and water-holding capacity (WHC) significantly superior to 1.5% and 2.5% NaCl controls. Enhanced WHC correlated with optimized water distribution, characterized by increased immobilized water and reduced free water. Mechanistically, U-E promoted hydrophobic interactions and disulfide bond formation while reducing free sulfhydryl groups. Fourier transform infrared spectroscopy confirmed enhanced β-sheet structures, and rheological analysis indicated maximal storage modulus and viscosity. Scanning electron microscopy revealed that UHP unfolded MP, exposing reactive sites for EWP integration via hydrophobic/disulfide linkages, forming a dense, uniform microstructure. This synergistic strategy effectively strengthens intermolecular forces, enabling the development of premium low-sodium shrimp surimi without compromising textural quality.
